name: monster-lair-small
summary: A single beast's den — a nest of debris, the remains of meals, the smell of it. Built into an existing dungeon room, not designed.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: irregular — creature chose the space, not an architect

primary_palette.note: humanoid beast (ogre, bugbear) — a fouled bed and a pile of gnawed bones
giant spider lair — the whole room is webbed
large mammalian beast — pressed-down straw and kill-leavings
hoarder-beast — shiny things taken from victims
ooze or grell lair — a single greasy depression in the floor

story_prompts: owlbear den in a ruined room — a door broken inward, a nest of timber and rag
spider lair where every cell is webbed floor to ceiling
gnoll lair — the victims aren't hidden, they're displayed
troll lair beside an underground stream for drinking
empty lair — the monster has been hunted and dragged out, only the nest remains

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: near-dark (0.1-0.25), creatures don't light their homes

anti_patterns: don't use tidy furniture — if there's a chair, it's broken
don't light the room well; lairs are dark unless the beast fears the dark
avoid props that imply civilized occupation (no books, no looms, no beds)
